title: Dependency / supply-chain audit + signed-release hardening
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. This recurring backlog task audits dependencies and their licenses and hardens release signing and supply-chain integrity (pinned lockfile, signed releases), keeping the project free of license-incompatible deps and of secrets.
objective: Audit dependencies and licenses and harden release signing and supply-chain integrity.
acceptanceCriteria: Audits dependencies and their licenses and removes/flags any incompatible dependency.
Hardens release signing and supply-chain integrity (pinned lockfile, signed releases); no secrets in code or artifacts; MIT-licensed.
output: A PR with the dependency/supply-chain audit results and signed-release hardening.
